rows.title: P0: SKILL Body vs Reference Boundary Re-Audit
rows.summary: v2.26.0 SKILL slim moved 36 protocols to references/. Codex dogfood revealed Blake skipped Layer 2 / completion report because those rules weren't loaded. Need per-reference judgment: keep execution-discipline rules in body (circular triggers), reference-ok for explicit-trigger protocols. Both platforms verified.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires auditing 36 reference files, classifying each, inlining ~200-300 lines, and verifying on two platforms — large scope.
rows.value_rationale: P0 quality-chain failure: agents silently skip mandatory gates because rules aren't loaded — directly blocks TAD protocol reliability.
rows.deliverable: Updated alex/blake SKILL.md files with ~200-300 lines of execution-discipline content inlined back from references/, plus a `skill-body-verify.sh` script confirming no circular-trigger protocols remain in references/.

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode Phase 2: Budget-Loop Auto-Execution + Safety Routing + Dogfood
rows.summary: Build surplus-execute.workflow.js — a budget-guarded loop that picks the top safe-to-YOLO candidate from the Phase 1 JSON sidecar, runs it through the TAD pipeline (yolo-epic.workflow.js), tracks spend, and stops when budget.remaining() < per_task_reserve. SAFETY-tagged tasks routed to needs-you list. Circuit breaker: max 2 retries → honest_partial → next. Concludes with a dogfood run on ≥1 real backlog task + SURPLUS-REPORT-{date}.md.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow file + SKILL execute-path + safety routing logic + dogfood run; depends on Phase 1.1 being complete first.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the full surplus-burn loop — turns idle quota into durable, reviewed deliverables autonomously; directly addresses the high-value O2 objective.
rows.deliverable: surplus-execute.workflow.js ships; a live dogfood run completes ≥1 real backlog task end-to-end and archives it; SURPLUS-REPORT-{date}.md produced; SAFETY-tagged tasks confirmed never auto-executed (verified by reviewing the needs-you list in the report).

rows.title: Distribute Pack-Quality Upgrades via *publish + *sync to 14 Projects
rows.summary: 21 upgraded packs (commits f2addac→9fd7975) + capability-upgrade Gate 2 + .agents parity are committed locally on main but NOT pushed or synced. User deferred 2026-06-13 pending more upgrades. When ready: read release-runbook, *publish then *sync, bundle v2.29.1 release.
rows.cost_rationale: Release + sync is a well-established runbook process; 2-4 files touched (runbook, sync script, version strings).
rows.value_rationale: Unblocks 14 downstream projects from receiving 21 pack upgrades; standard release cadence with high confidence of success.
rows.deliverable: GitHub tag v2.29.1 (or next version) pushed and all 14 downstream projects synced with `release-verify.sh structural` PASS ×14 confirming parity.

rows.title: Structured Feedback Collector — AI-generated HTML feedback interfaces for non-code artifacts
rows.summary: AI generates both the artifact AND an HTML feedback page decomposed into reviewable atomic elements. Human fills it out, exports JSON, AI applies precise targeted edits. Three working prototypes already exist in Colin voice project. Research complete (66 sources, 6 NotebookLM rounds). Key insight: generate any granularity of HTML feedback interface on-demand rather than a fixed GUI.
rows.cost_rationale: Large scope: requires a capability pack or skill for feedback-interface generation, plus integration with Gate 4; research is done but implementation is a new multi-file workflow.
rows.value_rationale: Directly addresses the hardest human-AI creative collaboration gap (feedback precision in non-code domains); aligns with 'judgment surface' repositioning; three proven prototypes validate feasibility.
rows.deliverable: .claude/skills/feedback-collector/SKILL.md with at least 3 capabilities (web-element overlay, general artifact decomposition, JSON-to-edit translation) + one working HTML feedback demo generated by the skill for a sample artifact.

rows.title: AI-Native Reading Companion Phase 4: Sinks + Multi-Format (PDF/TXT/URL)
rows.summary: Add durable, portable reading outputs (structured notes by section, open-question list, Markdown export of full session), spaced-retrieval quiz/flashcards via research-notebook, and format adapters for PDF, TXT, and URL so they feed the same reader pipeline. Depends on Phase 3 (live bridge) being complete — it is (Gate 4 accepted 2026-06-14).
rows.cost_rationale: Three new ingest adapter scripts + export-notes tool + SKILL.md modifications; PDF parsing complexity is non-trivial (scanned vs text fidelity decision needed).
rows.value_rationale: Closes the full reading companion loop — reading produces lasting knowledge artifacts and works on the most common real-world formats (PDF/URL), not just EPUB.
rows.deliverable: One command exports a session to Markdown (notes + questions + highlights); PDF and at least one of {TXT, URL} ingest into the same reader and pass Phase 2 reader ACs; quiz/flashcards generated from a finished reading — all verified by live ingest + export run on a real PDF and a URL.

rows.title: AI-Native Reading Companion — Phase 4 (Sinks + PDF/TXT/URL Support)
rows.summary: Phase 3 (live bridge) is ACCEPTED but Phase 4 (sinks + PDF/TXT/URL) is Planned. Deliverable code for Phase 3 is also UNCOMMITTED (untracked .claude/skills/reading-companion/). Carry-forward: real-browser visual co-read still UNVERIFIED.
rows.cost_rationale: New Epic phase requiring new abstraction (multi-format ingestion pipeline) and browser verification — large scope.
rows.value_rationale: Extends the reading companion to common document formats, making it practically usable for real books beyond EPUB; high strategic value as a generative non-dev use case.
rows.deliverable: Committed reading-companion skill files in .claude/skills/reading-companion/ plus Phase 4 implementation supporting PDF/TXT/URL input sources, with real-browser visual co-read verified (send→reply renders in enforcing-CSP browser).

rows.title: Trajectory Evaluation Harness — SLM-as-Judge for TAD Trace Quality
rows.summary: Build a lightweight eval harness (Epic C from O2/KR2 research) that reads JSONL traces and scores each step across 7 dimensions (CLEAR framework + Knowledge Activation + Gate Compliance) using Haiku as judge. Target: 0.80 Spearman vs human judgment on 20 sampled TAD traces. Directly validates whether Gate quality is improving over time without waiting for human post-mortems.
rows.cost_rationale: Novel harness + rubric design + fixture labeling across 3 files; requires a design spike before impl; L because it introduces a new abstraction layer.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1 — research round 3 ranked Trajectory Evaluation #3 (98% cost reduction, 0.87 accuracy at SLM scale); addresses 'validation theater' finding from YOLO audit (principles.md SAFETY entry); closes the gap between structural AC pass and actual behavioral quality improvement.
rows.deliverable: `.tad/hooks/lib/trajectory-judge.sh` (or `.py`) that ingests a trace JSONL file and emits a per-step score report; fixture harness with ≥5 labeled traces; Spearman correlation ≥0.80 against manually scored baseline written to `.tad/evidence/eval/trajectory-judge-baseline.md`.

rows.title: Fix detect_state Glob-Arm Hazard Before Next Version Bump
rows.summary: tad.sh 2.1*/2.2* arms (~305-313) will misclassify 3-part 2.19.x as v2.0 once TARGET_VERSION moves past 2.19.1. Must be addressed before bumping to next version.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-file tad.sh change; localized glob pattern fix.
rows.value_rationale: Time-sensitive: will cause silent misclassification on next version bump, potentially corrupting downstream project state detection.
rows.deliverable: Updated tad.sh detect_state function with correct 3-part version glob patterns, verified by running `bash tad.sh --detect-state` against a version string beyond 2.19.x and confirming correct classification.

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode Phase 1.1: Fix undated filename bug + AC1 verifier defect
rows.summary: Fix the SKILL-owned output path so *surplus --plan always writes SURPLUS-PLAN-{date}.md (not undated), and replace the false-failing node --check AC1 verifier with a wrapped-body check. Two P2 carry-forward defects from Phase 1.
rows.cost_rationale: Two isolated bug fixes in the surplus SKILL.md and workflow JS; no new abstractions needed.
rows.value_rationale: Correctness fix: every *surplus --plan run currently overwrites the same undated file, destroying plan history; the AC1 verifier false-fails every workflow AC.
rows.deliverable: SURPLUS-PLAN output path is date-stamped on every run (no more undated.md); AC1 verifier passes cleanly for all workflow files — verified by a live *surplus --plan invocation producing a correctly named artifact.

rows.title: Capability Pack Behavioral Examples — 3 Scenario Fixtures Per Pack
rows.summary: Add an `examples/` directory to each Capability Pack containing 3 scenario input + expected-output pairs, enabling before/after behavioral verification. Addresses the YOLO audit finding (Codex+Gemini both independently cited this gap: 'structural checks prove files exist correctly but do NOT prove the pack improves agent behavior'). Completes what quality leveling started: the pack structure is right, but there is still no behavioral proof.
rows.cost_rationale: 5 packs × 3 fixture pairs + SKILL update + installer change = 30+ files touched; high content-generation effort to write discriminative fixtures that don't just echo the pack rules.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1 — YOLO audit cross-model finding (Codex 3/5 + Gemini 2/5 both flagged validation theater as the top defect); closes the proof gap between 'pack installed' and 'pack changes agent behavior'; directly advances O2/KR1 evidence-backed upgrade directions.
rows.deliverable: 3 scenario fixture pairs (input.md + expected-output.md) in `examples/` for each of 5 highest-frequency packs (web-frontend, web-backend, product-thinking, code-security, ai-evaluation); `capability-upgrade` SKILL.md updated to require examples in Gate 2 AC; `install.sh` updated to deploy `examples/` to `.claude/skills/<pack>/examples/`.

rows.title: Close Edit-Time Parity Drift Window — add parity check to Blake completion protocol
rows.summary: The publish-time parity gate (step3b, Gate 4 PASS 2026-06-10) guarantees byte-parity at publish but the window between 'Blake edits a skill' and 'next *publish' remains open—Codex reads stale protocols in-repo during it. Fix: add `release-verify.sh parity` check to Blake's completion protocol after any handoff touching .claude/skills/; auto-copy on drift and include in the completion commit. Tool already hardened (e82704f).
rows.cost_rationale: Single file edit (~10 lines) in Blake's SKILL.md completion protocol to add the parity check call; no new abstraction needed.
rows.value_rationale: Closes a known residual gap recorded at Gate 4; parity tool already exists and is hardened—this is pure wiring, high confidence of success.
rows.deliverable: .claude/skills/blake/SKILL.md completion protocol includes parity check step; verified by grep for 'release-verify.sh parity' in blake completion section and a test handoff that touches a skill file triggers the auto-sync.

rows.title: TAD Repositioning — 'judgment surface' posture fixed into methodology layer + two fossil captures
rows.summary: Deep research (8 NotebookLM asks + Codex/Gemini adversarial) converged on a specific actionable next step: fix the 'no auto-evaluator → generate a judgment interface' reflex into the core methodology (alex/SKILL.md or project-knowledge), then document the hand-craft of a good judgment interface from the two existing fossils (playground + Colin voice AB player). README rewrite is explicitly parked (no head-to-head evidence yet). Rule-of-three: wait for a third domain before abstracting further.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope: one new judgment reflex rule in alex/SKILL.md or project-knowledge, plus a short documented pattern from two fossil cases (playground and Colin AB player).
rows.value_rationale: Grounds the repositioning in a concrete, exportable reflex rather than abstract positioning claims; directly improves how Alex responds to 'no auto-evaluator' situations across domains.
rows.deliverable: .tad/project-knowledge/patterns/judgment-surface.md capturing the 'no auto-evaluator → generate HTML judgment interface' pattern with two fossil examples; alex/SKILL.md references this pattern in the design-phase Socratic inquiry block.

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode — Phase 2: Budget-Loop Auto-Execution + Safety Routing
rows.summary: Phase 1 produced a live 53-candidate ranked plan + JSON sidecar. Phase 2 wires budget-loop auto-execution + safety routing + dogfood. JSON sidecar contract is ready.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow abstraction (budget-loop + safety router) touching multiple files; Epic-phase scope.
rows.value_rationale: Auto-execution of highest-value backlog work during surplus usage periods is the core value proposition of the Surplus Burn system.
rows.deliverable: Working *surplus auto-execution mode that reads the JSON sidecar, routes safe candidates to sub-agents for execution, and reports results — verified via a dogfood run executing ≥1 real safe candidate from the plan.

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode Phase 1.1: Filename Bug + AC1 node --check Invalid Fix
rows.summary: Phase 1.1 quick-fix: undated filename bug (date/output_path args didn't propagate; SKILL should own output filename). Also AC1 node --check verifier is invalid for all workflows + epic-audit.workflow.js:80 latent top-level-array schema bug.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches SKILL.md + 1-2 workflow files; moderate scope.
rows.value_rationale: Enables reliable surplus burn mode execution with correct output filenames; blocks Phase 2 auto-execution reliability.
rows.deliverable: Updated surplus SKILL.md owning output filename generation, fixed epic-audit.workflow.js:80 top-level-array schema, and AC1 verifier replaced with a valid check — all passing in a live *surplus --plan run producing a dated output file.

rows.title: O1/KR3 Gap Severity Assessment — Rate the 5 Identified Gaps by Impact
rows.summary: O1/KR3 status is 🔄 with 5+ gaps found but lacking the required impact rating. The gaps are identified (gate-ROI unproven, Staleness Trap, validation theater, rule soup/context saturation, zero collision detection) but no severity scoring exists. Produce a structured severity matrix: gap name, evidence source, impacted user count/scenario, severity (P0/P1/P2), remediation cost, and recommended fix direction. This completes KR3 and turns the raw gap list into an actionable triage document.
rows.cost_rationale: Synthesis from existing evidence files only; single output document; no code or infrastructure change; S because it is primarily a read-and-synthesize task.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR3 — the KR explicitly requires a severity assessment alongside gap identification; the gaps already exist in research files (repositioning-3-walls, YOLO audit findings in principles.md); this is the synthesis step that closes the KR and makes the list actionable for next Epic prioritization.
rows.deliverable: `.tad/evidence/research/2026-06-14-gap-severity-matrix.md` containing a severity-ranked table for ≥5 TAD capability gaps with evidence citations, impact ratings (P0/P1/P2), affected-scenario count, remediation direction, and OBJECTIVES.md KR3 status updated to ✅.

rows.title: Dream-Scanner Pass C Hardening (Dedup + Scope + fromjson Guard)
rows.summary: Three bundled follow-ups: (a) dedup new candidates against existing project-knowledge before emit; (b) file=null → override candidates mis-classify as project even when framework-scoped; (c) line ~183 fromjson-error on malformed context → guard leaks junk candidate.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches dream-scanner hook (2-3 functions) + 3 new fixtures; moderate change.
rows.value_rationale: Hardening the dream pipeline reduces noise and prevents junk candidates from polluting the evolve queue; moderate value as current workaround is human review.
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner hook with all 3 fixes, verified via 3 fixtures: (a) duplicate suppressed, (b) null-file framework override correctly classified, (c) malformed context yields no candidate (not junk).

rows.title: Local Skill Capture — *save-skill command to capture reusable patterns as project-level SKILL.md
rows.summary: Users discover reusable solutions in TAD projects but have no command to immediately capture them as structured skills. Proposal: *save-skill command where Alex extracts a SKILL.md draft from session context + user edits. Local skills go to .claude/skills/local/ (isolated from sync). Prerequisite was Domain Pack reliable loading (now complete with pack system unification).
rows.cost_rationale: Medium: *save-skill command in alex/SKILL.md, directory isolation scheme, sync protection verification — 3-4 files.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the bottom-up skill capture path; directly enables the skillify T1 local formalization tier identified in self-evolution-pruning epic; captures value from real project work at the right moment.
rows.deliverable: *save-skill command documented in alex/SKILL.md that generates a SKILL.md draft under .claude/skills/local/{slug}/ from session context; verified by running *save-skill in a test session and confirming the file appears at the correct path and is NOT touched by *sync dry-run.

rows.title: O3/KR3 Completion: Two More Deep-Ask Findings Rounds Saved to NotebookLM
rows.summary: O3/KR3 currently has 3 rounds saved against a target of ≥5. Run two focused deep-ask sessions on the remaining high-value open research questions: (Round 4) what is the measured ROI of structural validation gates vs soft-reminder gates in real multi-agent pipelines (directly speaks to the 2026-04-15 mechanical-enforcement decision reopened by depth-axis findings); (Round 5) what is the current state of agent escalation / uncertainty detection in production HITL systems. Save findings to `.tad/evidence/research/` per the research-notebook protocol.
rows.cost_rationale: Two research sessions using the research-engine workflow + saving findings = ~4 files; M because it spans multiple research cycles but no new infrastructure.
rows.value_rationale: O3/KR3 — objective explicitly targets ≥5 deep-ask findings saved (currently 3); Round 4 also advances O1/KR3 (gap severity: gate-ROI unproven was surfaced as a new gap in the repositioning stress-test findings); cross-objectives leverage makes this high-density.
rows.deliverable: Two findings files: `.tad/evidence/research/2026-06-14-gate-roi-vs-soft-reminder-findings.md` and `.tad/evidence/research/2026-06-14-escalation-uncertainty-detection-findings.md`; OBJECTIVES.md KR3 counter updated to 5; REGISTRY.yaml notebook entry updated with round count.

rows.title: TAD Unified Auth Status Layer — `tad auth status` cross-tool health check at session start
rows.summary: TAD depends on 4+ tools with heterogeneous auth (gh=keyring, codex=~/.codex, gemini=~/.gemini, NotebookLM=Playwright cookie). No unified check exists; auth failures discovered at point-of-use. Proposal: `tad auth status` command that queries all tools and outputs a unified table; integrated into Alex/Blake session start for early warning. Also: migrate tokens where possible to macOS keyring; fix NotebookLM `Aborted!` false positive.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope: new shell script + SessionStart hook integration + 4 per-tool probe commands; NotebookLM check is trickiest (browser-based).
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates recurring time-sink when auth fails mid-session; early-warning prevents wasted agent turns on auth errors.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/auth-status.sh returning a pass/fail table for gh/codex/gemini/notebooklm; SessionStart hook calls it and surfaces warnings; verified by running the script and seeing all 4 tools reported.

rows.title: TAD Opt-In Mode Posture — shrink CLAUDE.md from mandatory router to explicit-invoke
rows.summary: Redesign CLAUDE.md so TAD's Alex/Blake/Gate discipline activates only on explicit /alex /blake invocation, leaving capability packs auto-loading via native skill triggers. Parked due to one blocking concern: loosening the ambient default may erode strictness inside explicitly-invoked mode. Needs a design that guarantees explicit-invoke strictness is unaffected before proceeding.
rows.cost_rationale: 2-3 files to edit (CLAUDE.md, possibly alex/SKILL.md routing rules); needs a short testing cycle to verify strictness is preserved on explicit invoke.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces token tax and 'nagging' auto-routing failures on every casual interaction; aligns CLAUDE.md with real usage (packs already fully decoupled since 2026-06-11).
rows.deliverable: .claude/CLAUDE.md rewritten with opt-in posture + one surviving safety rail (pending-handoff scope-matched guard); grep-verifiable: CLAUDE.md no longer contains 'complex feature → 必须用 /alex' auto-route text.

rows.title: Capability Pack Codex YAML Adapter — auto-generate openai.yaml per pack
rows.summary: ECC places a 6-line agents/openai.yaml next to each SKILL.md for zero-cost Codex compatibility. Adding a --agent=codex flag to each pack's install.sh that auto-generates a minimal openai.yaml adapter would give TAD packs Codex compatibility without maintaining a separate AGENTS.md per pack. Small, well-scoped, prior art clear.
rows.cost_rationale: Small: one shared template + modification to install.sh; no new abstraction required, just templating.
rows.value_rationale: Small, concrete win for Codex parity across 22 packs without manual maintenance; ECC's 6-line format is well-understood prior art.
rows.deliverable: .tad/templates/openai-adapter.yaml.tmpl + install.sh --agent=codex flag that generates .agents/skills/{pack}/agents/openai.yaml from SKILL.md frontmatter; verified by running install.sh --agent=codex on one pack and confirming openai.yaml is generated with correct fields.

rows.title: Add Bash Command Deny Patterns to PreToolUse Hook
rows.summary: Pending: add deny patterns (rm -rf, DROP TABLE etc.) to the PreToolUse hook to prevent destructive commands from running without confirmation.
rows.cost_rationale: Single hook file modification with fixture; contained change.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents accidental destructive operations; safety-oriented improvement with clear concrete scope.
rows.deliverable: Updated PreToolUse hook script in .tad/hooks/ with deny patterns for rm -rf, DROP TABLE, and similar destructive commands, verified by a fixture showing the hook blocks the command and logs the attempt.

rows.title: SessionStart Full Component Health Check — validate hooks, config version, template integrity
rows.summary: TAD's SessionStart checks handoff/NEXT.md but not framework component integrity (hook scripts present, config version consistent, templates intact). Inspired by OpenHarness RuntimeBundle assembly. Failure discovered at point-of-use rather than startup.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium: extend session-start.sh with component presence checks + version consistency check; requires defining which components to check.
rows.value_rationale: Catches framework drift early (missing hook script, stale config version) before wasted agent turns; low-risk extension to an existing hook.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/component-health.sh that checks: all hook scripts in settings.json exist on disk, config.yaml version matches tad.sh version, required template files present; SessionStart calls it and outputs a warning table; verified by deleting a hook script and confirming the warning fires.

rows.title: IDEA: HTML-Based Structured Feedback Collector for Non-Code Artifacts
rows.summary: IDEA-20260610: AI generates artifact + HTML review interface; human gives structured feedback via HTML; JSON flows back to AI. Proven in Colin voice project (3 working prototypes). Complements the existing feedback_collector_protocol in Blake SKILL.
rows.cost_rationale: 2-3 files: HTML template + generator script + Blake SKILL update to reference it.
rows.value_rationale: Lowers friction for human feedback on non-code artifacts; builds on 3 proven prototypes and complements existing feedback collector protocol.
rows.deliverable: feedback-collector.html template (or generator script) producing a working HTML form for a given artifact type, with JSON export verified by a real dogfood run on a non-code artifact (e.g., a reading-companion session output).

rows.title: EPIC: Goal-Driven Research Director — Phase 3 (Research-Decision Loop)
rows.summary: 3/4 phases done. Phase 3 = Research-Decision Loop: decision traceability + --caller flag. Connects research findings to the decisions that triggered them for auditability.
rows.cost_rationale: Epic-phase scope touching research-engine workflow + SKILL + trace schema.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the research-to-decision traceability gap; completes the Epic and enables auditable research provenance.
rows.deliverable: Updated research-engine workflow/SKILL with --caller flag support and decision-traceability output, verified by a real research run where the resulting evidence file links back to the originating decision point.

rows.title: In-Session Git-Blame Knowledge Tool — Blake Temporal Reasoning on Project Rules
rows.summary: Equip Blake with a sandboxed `git-blame-knowledge` bash wrapper (scoped to `.tad/project-knowledge/`) so it can query WHY a project rule exists mid-session. When a Layer 1 retry is caused by an apparently inapplicable rule, Blake runs git-blame → git-log to find the originating handoff, then makes an informed judgment instead of blind follow or ignore. Addresses the root cause of stale-knowledge drift caught in Memory/Learning L2 pattern.
rows.cost_rationale: Single wrapper script + SKILL.md addition + fixture = 3 files; M because it modifies a SKILL body (SAFETY-adjacent boundary) but does not touch constraint rules.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1 — NotebookLM deep-ask ranked this #2 for in-session learning ROI (2-4 days effort); fills the 'static knowledge base' → 'queryable memory system' gap identified in research; maps to O2 direction #4 (Agent Memory Systems) from the research roadmap.
rows.deliverable: `.tad/hooks/lib/git-blame-knowledge.sh` wrapper (args: file, line-range; output: commit SHA, handoff name, date, change summary, ≤300 tokens); Blake SKILL.md step added: 'when Layer 1 retry caused by a project-knowledge rule, invoke git-blame-knowledge before escalating'; fixture test with ≥2 rule lookups producing correct handoff attribution.

rows.title: TAD Universal Methodology Skeleton — extract domain-agnostic Alex/Blake/Gate framework
rows.summary: Extract the Alex/Blake dual-agent + 4-Gate + knowledge accumulation skeleton as a domain-agnostic framework so TAD works for complex decision domains beyond software. ECC's Hermes operator shell proves market demand. TAD's competitive advantage over ECC's 246 flat skills is enforced process + knowledge accumulation. Key question: how much of Alex SKILL.md is software-specific vs domain-agnostic?
rows.cost_rationale: Large: requires auditing Alex/Blake SKILL.md for software-specific vs domain-agnostic content, abstracting terminology, and producing a parameterized skeleton; many open design questions.
rows.value_rationale: Enables TAD to address the 'no auto-evaluator' domain gap identified in repositioning research; aligns with the 'TAD Universal Method' strategic direction with 195K-star ECC proving market demand.
rows.deliverable: .tad/methodology-skeleton/SKELETON.md containing the domain-agnostic Alex/Blake/Gate protocol with substitution variables documented (e.g. {validator} replaces tsc/jest); an audit table mapping each SKILL.md section to software-specific vs universal.

rows.title: Run *evolve Cross-Project (5 Projects, 50+ Trace Files)
rows.summary: 5 projects have trace files (50+ total). Run *evolve cross-project to surface framework-scope improvements from multi-project patterns that single-project *optimize would miss.
rows.cost_rationale: Multi-project read + synthesis; touches traces across 2-4 directories.
rows.value_rationale: Cross-project trace analysis can surface systemic TAD friction points invisible in single-project views; aligns with core *evolve purpose.
rows.deliverable: *evolve run producing ≥2 framework-level dream candidates in .tad/evidence/traces/ with multi-project pattern evidence, ready for next *dream scan.

rows.title: Fix Multi-Table §11 Decision Parser Re-bind Bug
rows.summary: emit_decision_points locks havehdr on the FIRST Decision Summary table and never re-binds → 2nd+ decision tables in one §11 silently dropped; trailing non-Decision table over-emits with stale indices. Fix = re-bind havehdr whenever a fresh row's cells are decision+chosen.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-file hook fix with a new fixture; contained change under 30k tokens.
rows.value_rationale: Low priority — multi-table §11 is rare in practice; correctness fix but not blocking any current workflow.
rows.deliverable: Updated emit_decision_points function in the trace hook (likely .tad/hooks/) with re-bind logic, plus a fixture demonstrating a 2-table §11 correctly emitting events from both tables (exit 0 on valid, exit 1 on stale-index over-emit).

rows.title: Fix classify_scope Word-Boundary Slug Matching
rows.summary: Unbounded substring globs *hook*/*trace*/*registry* false-classify project slugs as framework (webhook-handler→framework, registry-of-products→framework). Fix = bracket-class word-boundary per architecture.md 2026-04-24.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-file fix in dream-scanner hook; straightforward pattern substitution.
rows.value_rationale: Low risk of false framework fan-out in *evolve; correctness fix but low frequency impact since human_override is rare.
rows.deliverable: Updated classify_scope function in dream-scanner hook using word-boundary patterns, with a fixture demonstrating webhook-handler correctly classifies as project (not framework).

rows.title: TAD Self-Test Agent — automated sub-agent verification of core TAD behavior
rows.summary: TAD functional verification is entirely manual. Proposal: spawn a sub-agent via Agent tool that inherits CLAUDE.md/hooks/skills, reads config files to verify integrity, checks SessionStart output, and reports PASS/FAIL. Useful for post-upgrade regression testing and post-sync downstream verification.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium: test script defining check cases + a skill or handoff invoking the sub-agent pattern; open question on whether nested Skill invocation works.
rows.value_rationale: Automates the most common post-upgrade verification; sub-agent inheritance from Claude Code is confirmed working; reduces manual testing overhead on each TAD version bump.
rows.deliverable: .tad/tests/self-test.md containing a checklist-driven test script that a sub-agent can execute; verified by spawning it and getting a PASS report that includes: config integrity check, hook existence check, one skill load verification.

rows.title: IDEA: TAD Universal Methodology Skeleton (Domain-Agnostic Process)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Extract the TAD methodology as a domain-agnostic process skeleton usable by non-developers (e.g., product managers, researchers). Aligns with the TAD Universal Method direction noted in project memory.
rows.cost_rationale: New document requiring synthesis of existing TAD principles into domain-agnostic framing; 2-3 files.
rows.value_rationale: Strategic direction for TAD expansion beyond software dev; aligns with user goal of building AI agents in non-dev domains.
rows.deliverable: A methodology-skeleton.md document (or equivalent SKILL.md) describing the TAD 4-gate + 2-agent process in domain-agnostic terms with ≥3 worked examples from non-dev domains (e.g., research, product, content creation).

rows.title: IDEA: Automated TAD Behavior Validation Agent
rows.summary: IDEA-20260401: Automated agent that validates TAD protocol behavior (e.g., verifies agents follow Gate sequences, don't skip Layer 2, emit correct trace events) without requiring a human to run the full TAD flow manually.
rows.cost_rationale: New abstraction requiring definition of behavioral test harness; Epic-phase scope.
rows.value_rationale: Would catch protocol regressions automatically; high leverage if TAD continues evolving rapidly, but design is underspecified.
rows.deliverable: tad-self-test.sh or tad-self-test.workflow.js that runs ≥5 behavioral checks against a real TAD session transcript and exits 0 when all pass, 1 when any protocol violation is detected.

rows.title: Express Tier: Durable Frontmatter Marker
rows.summary: Slug-naming convention still false-WARNs any express handoff that forgets the name. Durable fix = express: true frontmatter consumed by layer2-audit (vs slug-as-proxy).
rows.cost_rationale: Touches layer2-audit script + handoff template + potentially alex/blake SKILL for frontmatter spec.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces false-WARN noise in layer2-audit for express handoffs; quality-of-life improvement for frequent express workflows.
rows.deliverable: Updated layer2-audit script to read express: true frontmatter field, plus updated handoff template with the new field, verified via fixture showing an express handoff without 'express' in slug correctly detected as express tier.

rows.title: Citation Pointer Audit for 8 Agent-Adjacent Packs
rows.summary: findings.md reports carry a top source-list AND a report-body reference list with DIFFERENT [N] numbering; pack Source: [N] tags may point to wrong URLs (claims are correct, pointers ambiguous). Audit + pin URLs for all 8 packs.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches 8 pack reference directories; moderate scope across multiple files.
rows.value_rationale: Correctness of source citations matters for pack credibility; moderate value as claims are already correct, only pointers are ambiguous.
rows.deliverable: Updated source citation tags in all 8 agent-adjacent pack files (.claude/skills/{rag-retrieval,agent-memory,llm-observability,ai-guardrails,data-engineering,agent-orchestration,synthetic-data,knowledge-graph}/references/) with correct URL pinning, verified by spot-checking ≥3 citations per pack.

rows.title: IDEA: Deprecate Domain Pack YAML Format Entirely
rows.summary: IDEA-20260508: Formally deprecate the YAML Domain Pack format now that Capability Packs (SKILL.md) have replaced them. Pack System Unification Phase 1 retired 9 YAML packs; this idea covers completing the deprecation.
rows.cost_rationale: Single deprecation.yaml entry + small tad.sh guard + doc update; contained change.
rows.value_rationale: Cleanup/hygiene; Pack System Unification already did the heavy lifting; this finalizes the deprecation trail.
rows.deliverable: Updated deprecation.yaml with formal YAML Domain Pack end-of-life entry, updated tad.sh to error/warn if any .tad/domains/*.yaml files are detected, and documentation update in MULTI-PLATFORM.md confirming YAML packs are fully retired.

rows.title: Cross-Project Skill Harvest — *harvest scan of registered projects' local skills for T2/T3 promotion
rows.summary: After local skills accumulate across projects (via Epic 2 / *save-skill), *harvest scans all registered projects, generates a cross-project index, and enables review/promotion through T1/T2/T3 tiers (defined in self-evolution-pruning epic). Requires local skill capture (IDEA-20260407-local-skill-capture) to have generated data. Self-evolution-pruning epic explicitly referenced this idea as the 'reflux channel' fix.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium: *harvest command in alex/SKILL.md, scan logic across sync-registry.yaml projects, collision detection for slug duplicates.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the reflux channel identified as breakpoint 3 in self-evolution-pruning; enables graduated promotion of proven patterns to TAD master; prerequisite is local skill capture being implemented first.
rows.deliverable: *harvest command in alex/SKILL.md that reads sync-registry.yaml project list, scans each project's .tad/active/skillify-candidates/ directory, and outputs a ranked cross-project index table grouped by slug; verified by running *harvest and seeing entries from at least one downstream project.

rows.title: Unified Agent Adapter Pattern — auto-detect and invoke TAD across Codex/Gemini/Cursor
rows.summary: Build a unified agent detection + invocation protocol layer so TAD can run on Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, etc. Inspired by html-anything's detect.ts (17-agent auto-detect via PATH scan + 3 protocols: stdin/argv/argv-message). Key difference: TAD needs multi-turn + file read/write protocols, not single-round HTML generation. Research grounded in deep-ask findings.
rows.cost_rationale: Large: new detection script, per-agent protocol adapters for 3+ runtimes, degradation tier logic, integration testing across platforms.
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates per-platform manual invocation guides; enables TAD Universal Method direction; but TAD's multi-turn requirements make protocol design significantly harder than html-anything's single-turn case.
rows.deliverable: .tad/agents/detect.sh that auto-detects available runtimes (claude-code/codex/gemini) and outputs a tier (1a/1b/2/3); plus .tad/agents/{runtime}-adapter.md for each detected platform with verified invocation commands.

rows.title: GitAgent Protocol (GAP) Export for TAD Cross-Platform Portability
rows.summary: TAD's .tad/ directory structure maps closely to GitAgent Protocol's agent format (agent.yaml, memory/, skills/, hooks/). Exporting TAD as a GAP-compatible repo would let TAD skills and knowledge run on non-Claude runtimes (Cursor, OpenClaw, Codex, Gemini CLI) without per-platform adaptation. GAP is v0.1.0 spec — stability risk. TAD already has Codex-edition skills (.tad/codex/) which is a partial version of this pattern.
rows.cost_rationale: Large: requires mapping TAD's config module system to GAP's simpler agent.yaml, deciding how to handle Alex/Blake dual-agent in a single-agent spec, and verifying cross-runtime execution.
rows.value_rationale: Enables TAD methodology portability across runtimes; aligns with 'TAD as universal method' direction; but GAP spec is still v0.1 and may not be stable enough to build against.
rows.deliverable: .tad/gap-export/agent.yaml + .tad/gap-export/skills/ + .tad/gap-export/hooks/ that passes GAP validator (gitagent validate command); at least one downstream runtime (Cursor or OpenClaw) successfully loads and runs a basic TAD skill from the export.

rows.title: Semantic Dedup for Dream-Scanner Candidates
rows.summary: Current grep-on-.decision/.chosen dedup is inert (0/31 real values match). Needs title/discovery match or embedding-based semantic dedup to prevent duplicate dream candidates surfacing repeatedly.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires design decision (title-match vs embedding) and touches 2-3 hook files.
rows.value_rationale: Dedup failure causes candidate noise in dream/evolve pipeline; value is moderate since human review already filters duplicates.
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner Pass C with title/discovery-based dedup logic (or embedding stub), verified via a fixture with 2 semantically-duplicate candidates showing only 1 emitted.

rows.title: Hook Timeout Configuration — add timeout_seconds to hook definitions
rows.summary: TAD hooks have no timeout control; a hanging hook script blocks indefinitely. OpenHarness defines per-hook-type timeouts (command/http/prompt: 30s, agent: 60s). First need to verify whether Claude Code's native hook runner already implements timeouts.
rows.cost_rationale: Small: add timeout_seconds fields to settings.json hook definitions (if Claude Code supports it) or wrap hook scripts with shell timeout command.
rows.value_rationale: Small safety improvement; prevents runaway hooks from blocking sessions; but Claude Code may already handle this natively.
rows.deliverable: All hook entries in .claude/settings.json include timeout_seconds field (or documented finding that Claude Code runtime already enforces timeouts natively, citing source); verified by checking settings.json grep for timeout.

rows.title: Run *optimize on menu-snap (14 Trace Files)
rows.summary: 14 trace files from menu-snap project are available for *evolve/*optimize analysis. Run *optimize to analyze execution patterns and surface framework improvements.
rows.cost_rationale: Single *optimize invocation; read-only analysis producing a report.
rows.value_rationale: Harvest framework insights from existing trace data; value depends on richness of traces and whether patterns surface actionable improvements.
rows.deliverable: *optimize run completion report in .tad/evidence/ listing ≥3 concrete framework improvement candidates derived from menu-snap trace analysis.

rows.title: Test Agent Teams on Next Full or Standard TAD Task
rows.summary: Pending item to test Agent Teams capability on a real Full or Standard TAD task to validate the feature works end-to-end.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires a real TAD task execution with Agent Teams enabled; touches agent orchestration files.
rows.value_rationale: Validates Agent Teams integration with TAD; moderate value as it's a testing task rather than new capability.
rows.deliverable: Completion report documenting Agent Teams behavior on a real TAD task: which agents were spawned, whether they completed correctly, and any protocol deviations observed.

rows.title: Config Environment Variable Override — TAD_MODEL / TAD_CONFIG_PATH env layer
rows.summary: TAD config has only a YAML file layer with no env-variable override. OpenHarness supports 4 priority layers (default → file → env → CLI). Primarily useful for CI/CD or containerized scenarios. TAD is currently local CLI only — value is low unless there's a real CI/CD use case.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium: requires changing config loading logic in whichever script reads config.yaml + naming convention decision + documentation.
rows.value_rationale: Adds standard config flexibility but TAD's single-user CLI context has no actual CI/CD deployment need today; low practical value unless TAD expands to multi-user.
rows.deliverable: .tad/hooks/lib/load-config.sh (or equivalent) that reads TAD_MODEL and TAD_CONFIG_PATH env vars and overrides config.yaml values; verified by running `TAD_MODEL=claude-opus-4 tad.sh` and confirming the override is reflected in startup output.

rows.title: IDEA: TAD as Opt-In Mode (CLAUDE.md Posture Flip)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260613: Flip TAD from default-always-on to opt-in mode in CLAUDE.md. PARKED due to concern that loosening default may erode explicit-invoke strictness. Revisit when evidence supports.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches CLAUDE.md + alex/blake SKILL.md for detection; moderate scope but high uncertainty on design.
rows.value_rationale: PARKED by design — user concern about eroding strictness makes this low-confidence; potential value if it reduces friction for simple tasks.
rows.deliverable: Updated CLAUDE.md with opt-in TAD mode syntax + updated alex/blake SKILL.md opt-in detection logic, with a written rationale document explaining why the posture flip does/doesn't erode strictness.

rows.title: Domain Pack Monthly Refresh — tools-registry + skills health check cadence
rows.summary: Domain Pack tools and skills research go stale. Proposal: monthly health check scanning tool versions, searching for better alternatives, updating registry. NOTE: Domain Packs in YAML format are being retired (pack-system-unification epic). This idea may be partially superseded — the value shifts to Capability Pack refresh cadence, not YAML packs.
rows.cost_rationale: Small if reframed as a Capability Pack freshness cadence note in project-knowledge; Large if building automation.
rows.value_rationale: Still relevant for Capability Pack freshness (packs reference specific tool versions/patterns that go stale), but original scope (YAML domain packs) is being retired.
rows.deliverable: .tad/project-knowledge/patterns/pack-refresh-cadence.md documenting the trigger conditions (tool version bump, >90 days since last research) and a checklist for refreshing a Capability Pack's references/ files using the pack-upgrade workflow.

rows.title: DeerFlow Patterns — evaluate sandbox isolation + persistent memory for TAD
rows.summary: DeerFlow 2.0 (ByteDance, 45K stars) has three patterns TAD lacks: container sandbox isolation per agent, two-layer persistent memory (session + cross-session user profile), and multi-channel Message Gateway (Slack/Telegram/Feishu). TAD does some things better (Quality Gates, Domain Pack, Human-as-bridge). This is a research/evaluation idea rather than a specific deliverable.
rows.cost_rationale: Large if pursuing any of the three patterns seriously; Small if producing just an evaluation report.
rows.value_rationale: Sandbox isolation and persistent memory are relevant to TAD's long-term architecture, but adoption requires significant design work and may conflict with the single-user CLI principle.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/deerflow-patterns-eval.md containing a go/no-go recommendation for each of the three DeerFlow patterns (sandbox, persistent memory, message gateway) with specific TAD integration design sketch for any 'go' items.

rows.title: Domain Pack Taxonomy Reorganization — vertical vs cross-cutting classification
rows.summary: 21 Domain Packs organized vertically but some are cross-cutting. Proposal: add cross_cutting:true tag + README index. Demoted by user in 2026-04-27 ('no concrete pain point = over-engineering'). Domain Packs are now being retired (pack-system-unification epic). This idea is largely obsolete.
rows.cost_rationale: Small if just adding a tag; but the domain being retired makes any effort here wasted.
rows.value_rationale: Domain Packs being retired renders the taxonomy question moot; even if not retired, user explicitly demoted this as 'over-engineering' with no concrete pain point.
rows.deliverable: Confirmed obsolescence: check whether .tad/domains/ has been archived by pack-system-unification; if so, close this idea as obsolete with an archive note.
